entry.title: SAP Concur Integration: Follow SAP Activate Roadmap for Your Cloud Transformation
entry.content:
For customers upgrading or migrating their SAP Concur integration (SAP ICS)—whether from SAP ECC to SAP S/4HANA or from SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud (RISE) to Public Cloud (GROW)—following the SAP Activate Roadmap. is strongly recommended.

SAP Activate provides standardized, solution‑specific guidance for planning and executing ERP transformations, including integration‑relevant activities and quality gates. The SAP Activate Roadmap Viewer is available via SAP for Me and helps align SAP Concur Integration decisions with the overall S/4HANA migration approach. 

As part of SAP Activate, customers should rely on Fit‑to‑Standard and integration workshops to:

  • Validate existing on-premise‑based integration patterns
  • Align with supported SAP ICS capabilities in S/4HANA
  • Understand differences between Private Cloud and Public Cloud integration models


Official Documentation and SAP Notes

During both planning and execution, we strongly recommend relying on official SAP documentation to ensure a consistent, supported approach.
SAP Activate Roadmap Viewer 

The SAP Activate Roadmap provides structured, solution‑specific guidance, including relevant activities and quality gates. Recommended roadmaps include:

  • SAP Activate for SAP Concur
  • SAP Activate for SAP Cloud ERP Private Edition
  • SAP Activate for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

SAP Integration with Concur Solutions for S/4HANA Cloud
  • SAP Help Portal Documentation for SAP Integration with Concur Solutions 

Key Documentation and SAP Notes for SAP Concur Integration

    • 3140635 – Release Information: SAP S/4HANA Cloud and the Concur Integration
    • 2922806 – FAQ: General Information about the Concur Integration
    • 2924469 – FAQ: Concur Integration (S/4HANA Cloud)
    • 3483312 – SAP Concur Integration: Transitioning from SAP ECC or SAP S/4HANA
                             to SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud

Need Help?

If you’re looking for guidance on SAP Concur integration (SAP ICS) as part of your cloud transformation journey, our integration team is here to support you: sapconcurintegration@sap.com

entry.title: Re: Print via output control indicator - Goods movement
entry.content:
Hello @Moon3

Thank you for the clarification.

Since you are using MIGO, the default value of Print via Output Control is not controlled only by Output Parameter Determination. OPD controls whether the output can be determined once the transaction data matches the rule, but the default selection of the checkbox in MIGO is linked to the MIGO print indicator behaviour and the user parameter NDR.

Compare the user master parameter in DEV and TEST.  Make sure in your Parameters tab, you maintain parameter ID NDR with value X. Save, log off and log on again. Open MIGO and check whether Print via Output Control is manually selected.

Regarding transport configuration, I would mainly compare these items between DEV and TEST:

  • Output Parameter Determination – Goods Movement – Output Type, Receiver, Channel, Printer Settings, Form Template
  • Print Active, Print Item, Print Version, Movement Type, whether all OPD rules are activated in your Test environment and whether the relevant software collection or configuration transport was successfully imported into your TEST environment.

However, if all I mentioned above are truly identical, then it would be an OPD transport issue. Hence why you should first check the NDR user parameter behaviour in your TEST environment.

If the checkbox is now automatically selected, then the root cause was the missing or inconsistent user parameter in you TEST environment. You can compare the full OPD setup and raise a case with SAP on this matter. You may reference KBAs 3079393 and 3741385 while doing that.

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Accrual Management in SAP S4 Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hi All,

We are implementing SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud (PSA) for a professional services firm. As part of the month‑end close, the client must post accruals to reflect project costs incurred but not yet received (Supplier Invoices not loaded to the project)—examples include travel and accommodation for internal and external staff assigned to projects. A core requirement is that these accruals should reverse automatically once the actual costs are posted. Our proposed approach is to use Accrual Management (the accrual engine) for PO‑based accruals, and to use the “Post General Journal Entries” app with the standard template (which supports a planned reversal date) for manual accruals. In addition to this, would accruals drive revenue recognition as well similar to actual cost. We welcome advice from the community on any additional SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud functionality or alternative approaches to meet this requirement.

Appreciate your comment and feedback.

Thank you

entry.title: SAP Community Voice: Updates based on your Feedback, SAP experts Blog Posts and SAP Sapphire 2026
entry.content:
Explore our latest technical blog posts covering SAP Fiori annotations, People Intelligence in SAP Business Data Cloud, and automation tips for SAP Build. Plus find out more details about a new graphical user interface now available directly in the SAP Business Technology Platform cockpit. 

We also want to highlight several platform enhancements that are now live. These changes were developed based on the feedback and discussions happening in our forums and comments; our team has been working to address these needs and create a more intuitive experience. 

Let´s dive in!

Featured Blog Posts 

SAP Fiori Elements: Essentials for XML Annotations 

Working with SAPUI5 Fiori Elements often means relying on annotation examples from the UI5 Demo Kit, but simply copying those snippets can quickly lead to frustration when they don't work as expected. SAP Champion Mio Yasutake explains in her latest blog post exactly why that happens, focusing on two key aspects: correctly defining the annotation target and including the proper OData vocabulary reference. Using clear CAP and RAP examples, Mio shows how to extract the right values from metadata.xml and adapt documentation samples step by step into a working annotation.xml. It's a very practical guide that helps developers move from trial-and-error to a more structured and confident approach when working with annotations. 

Read Mio Yasutake blog post here  

People Intelligence in SAP Business Data Cloud 

Traditionally, consultants relied on SAP SuccessFactors OData APIs to extract HR data into tools like SAP Datasphere and SAP Analytics Cloud, which required significant modeling effort and could lead to performance challenges as data volumes increased. In this blog post, SAP expert Zili Zhou demonstrates how People Intelligence, delivered as part of SAP Business Data Cloud, replaces this approach with a native, pre-built analytical framework that hides API complexity, improves data consistency, and provides a near real-time, end-to-end view of the workforce. The post walks through the full configuration process across BDC Cockpit, SuccessFactors, Datasphere, and SAC, including activating data packages, installing intelligent applications, setting up data access control, and validating reports. We highly recommend checking the full post, because in the end you will also find the glossary with valuable links 

Read Zili Zhou blog post here  

Tips on using SAP Build Process Automation 

Instead of triggering integrations manually, this blog post by SAP Community member Ajay Wandre shows a practical way to automate the process by combining SAP Build Process Automation with SAP Integration Suite. Using a step-by-step approach, Ajay demonstrates how to trigger a CPI iFlow through an automation job-starting with creating and activating an iFlow, configuring destinations in SAP Business Technology Platform, and building actions and automation artifacts inside SAP Build. The post walks through the full setup, including deployment, Desktop Agent configuration, and scheduling triggers, ultimately showing how CPI iFlows can be executed automatically and monitored with response payloads-making it a useful example of how to streamline integration processes using automation. 

Read Ajay Wandre blog post here 

First graphical user interface directly from the BTP Cockpit 

Following the initial release of the Application Vulnerability Report API, this blog by SAP expert Fabian Richter introduces a major enhancement: a new graphical user interface now available directly in the SAP Business Technology Platform cockpit. Designed to complement the existing API-based approach, the UI provides a more intuitive and accessible way for developers, DevOps engineers, and security teams to understand and act on open-source vulnerabilities in Cloud Foundry applications. With features like a centralized findings dashboard, severity breakdown by subaccount, and detailed views including CVSS scores, CVEs, and remediation guidance, the tool enables faster prioritization and resolution of security issues. By combining SAP's existing scanning capabilities with a visual layer, this update makes vulnerability management more actionable across the entire BTP landscape. Make sure to read the full content and to try out the UI and give your feedback in the comments. The team would appreciate hearing if the dashboard gave you the overview you needed and what you would like to see next.  

Read Fabian Richter blog post here 

Community News 

SAP Community Enhancements 

Over the past few months, our team has been diving deep into your comments and listening closely to the discussions happening across our community. We have heard your feedback loud and clear: you wanted a platform that feels more intuitive, keeps content fresh, and prioritizes the needs of the people using it every day. We’re thrilled to roll out these updates, shaped directly by your feedback, and particularly excited to debut the 60-day 'Recent Activity' filter!  

Find more details about each one of these new enhancements here: 

Improved Content Discovery in Recent Activity Feeds 

Recent Activity feeds across the main interaction styles have been refined to highlight more relevant and timely content: 

  • Most viewed, most commented, and most liked filters now focus on recently created content (last 60 days), instead of pulling very old posts. 
  • This ensures that highly engaged recent discussions are easier to find. 

Easier Joining and Following (Even When Not Logged In) 

New and anonymous visitors are now better guided to engage with the community: 

  • Join Group buttons are now visible even when you are not logged in 
    If you click “Join” while logged out, you’ll be prompted to log in or register, and the join action will complete automatically after login. 
  • Subscribe buttons are now visible when logged out 
    On tag pages, users can immediately see the option to follow a topic, encouraging discovery and return visits.  

A Cleaner, More Relevant Events Calendar 

We’ve improved how events appear in the main Community Events Calendar: 

  • Events created within Location Groups are now more selectively promoted to the main "All Location Events" community calendar 
  • The main calendar focuses on broader, community‑relevant location events 
  • All Group events remain fully visible inside their respective Groups 
  • Existing event boards remain fully visible 

Better Read / Unread Visibility 

To help you keep track of what’s new: 

  • Read / unread indicators are now available for Blog Posts in tile view, extending the functionality already available in other feeds. 

Clearer Dates and More Consistent Formatting 

Several refinements improve clarity and consistency: 

  • “Last edited” dates now respect your personal date format preferences 
    This applies especially to Knowledge Base (TKB) content. 
  • Accepted solution indicators are now consistently aligned, even when titles span multiple lines. 

Cleaner Homepage and Navigation Adjustments 

To reduce clutter and improve focus: 

  • The “users online” statistic has been removed from the homepage, simplifying the layout. 
  • Content building blocks (CCB widgets) were repositioned to better highlight important announcements now that carousels have been removed. 

Polished UI Fixes and Small Improvements 

A set of targeted refinements improves visual quality and usability: 

  • Improved alignment and spacing in notification settings 
  • Minor icon and layout adjustments for better consistency 
  • Fixes to ensure interface elements behave the same way across pages 

Read the SAP Community Enhancements blog post here for more details  

As always, please make sure to try out these updates and give us your feedback!  

Events 

SAP Sapphire 2026

SAP Sapphire 2026 is right around the corner, and we are very excited to see how our SAP community is going to be present and engaged in the sessions and meetings. In case you need more reasons to be excited, our SAP expert Anja Rosker has posted an amazing blog post about how the SAP runs SAP track centers on a straightforward question: what can be learned from how SAP operates its own business using its own technology?  

Across Orlando and Madrid, the program brings together more than 50 sessions and over 16 hours of content, led by SAP’s internal IT experts who are directly involved in running these systems. Rather than focusing on vision, the sessions provide visibility into how agents, applications, and data are applied in a real enterprise environment, covering areas such as finance, HR, procurement, customer experience, operations, and much more. Beyond the sessions, the experience extends into interactive formats like the Experience Hub, the Expert Bar, and the C-suite Experience. SAP Sapphire 2026 offers a chance to engage directly with the people and experiences behind it. Find out more in the session catalogs: Orlando and Madrid. And get a first exciting taste of what’s ahead in the short YouTube teaser videos. 

Read Anja Rosker blog post here 

SAP Community-driven events calendar: May 2026  

The first quarter of 2026 was remarkable for all the community-driven events that have happened all over the world. And we are very grateful and excited for another month filled with more opportunities for our SAP community members to get together!  

Check out now our May 2026 list: 

  • Lithuanian SAP Community Event on the 7th of May, 2026 
  • SAP Stammtisch Wien - 07.05.2026 - SAP Community 
  • SAP Stammtisch Munich (05/2026) - SAP Community 
  • Quedada SAP en Cervecería Köln - SAP Community
  • SAP Community Stammtisch Chemnitz 
  • SAP Stammtisch | SAP Community Meetup (Cologne #7) 
  • SAP Inside Track CErrado Cerrado 2026 
  • SAP Inside Track Melbourne - 20th May 2026 
  • Meetup - Sapphire Madrid 2026 - SAP Community 
  • SAP Sydney Stammtisch May '26 - SAP Community 
  • SAP Stammtisch in Basel - SAP Community 
  • SAP Essentials - workshops for everyone! - SAP Community 
  • SAP Inside Track Wroclaw 2026 - SAP Community 
  • SAP Inside Track Recife 2026 - Edição dos 10 anos ... - SAP Community 
  • SAP Stammtisch CDMX (Santa Fe) - CodeJams Latam 2... - SAP Community 
  • SAP Community CPH Meetup - 28th May 2026 - SAP Community 
  • SAP Stammtisch Monterrey - CodeJams Latam 2026 - SAP Community 
  • BTP in Rio - SAP Community

entry.title: Re: Accrual Management in SAP S4 Public Cloud
entry.content:
This is a solid approach for a Professional Services (PSA) environment, @nikesh_rathodeb6b33. Using the Accrual Engine for PO-based items and the Post General Journal Entries app for manual reversals is the standard "Clean Core" recommendation for S/4HANA Public Cloud.

To answer your question on Revenue Recognition: In the PSA scenario (specifically with Event-Based Revenue Recognition - EBRR), accruals posted via the Accrual Engine typically do not automatically drive revenue recognition like actual costs do. EBRR usually triggers based on actual cost postings to the project (WBS element). If you need these accruals to impact revenue, you may need to look into Manual Accruals for EBRR or ensure the account assignment logic in your Accrual Engine is tightly integrated with the project’s recognition key.

One alternative to consider is the Purchase Order Accruals functionality within the Accrual Engine, which can automate the "calculate-review-post" cycle more effectively than manual journal entries for recurring travel costs. Also, ensure your integration seams are validated—perhaps through Tosca API Testing—to confirm that the reversal logic doesn't create "orphan" entries in your project's financial ledger during the month-end close.

entry.title: Re: Its 2025 , yet recurring absence doesnt respect take...
entry.content:
This is a known architectural gap that continues to impact high-compliance environments, @IMZEI. Since the system processes recurring absences as discrete, independent objects, any "Take Rule" relying on a Count or Frequency check across a period fails to see the "set" as a whole.

While there isn't a direct fix on the immediate roadmap to change the validation engine's logic for recurring sets, many organizations use Integration Center or a Custom MDF Alert as a workaround. You can schedule a job to scan for "Abuse of Type" (e.g., more than X occurrences in Y weeks) and flag these for HR review after the fact.

To maintain a "Clean Core," I'd also recommend using SAP Cloud ALM to document this limitation in your business process and potentially leverage Tosca API Testing to validate that any custom middleware you build to "catch" these occurrences remains stable during SuccessFactors releases.

entry.title: CL_SALV_BS_RUNTIME_INFO returns empty data after upgrade to S/4HANA 2025
entry.content:
Description:
After upgrading S/4HANA On-Premise from 1809 to 2025, part of our custom code no longer works.

We are using CL_SALV_BS_RUNTIME_INFO to capture ALV output from a standard report. The logic worked correctly in 1809.

Simplified code:

cl_salv_bs_runtime_info=>set(
display = abap_false
metadata = abap_false
data = abap_true ).

SUBMIT fagl_line_item_browser
VIA SELECTION-SCREEN
AND RETURN.

TRY.
cl_salv_bs_runtime_info=>get_data_ref(
IMPORTING r_data = lt_data_ref ).
CATCH cx_salv_bs_sc_runtime_info.
MESSAGE 'Error when receiving data.' TYPE 'E'.
ENDTRY.

After the upgrade, lt_data_ref is always initial (empty), although the report itself displays data correctly when executed normally.

It seems that FAGL_LINE_ITEM_BROWSER might now be using ALV with IDA (HANA-based rendering) instead of classical SALV, which could explain why CL_SALV_BS_RUNTIME_INFO no longer captures the output data.

Has the behavior of CL_SALV_BS_RUNTIME_INFO changed in S/4HANA 2025, or is there a recommended alternative approach to retrieve ALV data from FAGL_LINE_ITEM_BROWSER in this case?

entry.title: Re: S/4HANA AIF enhancement for Order to Cash
entry.content:
This is a great candidate for AIF Value Mapping, @MuralitharanC. To keep your core stable, I recommend using Transaction /AIF/VMAP to create a regional mapping for the AUART field. By defining a transformation at the AIF Structure Mapping level, you can intercept the incoming web payload and switch the order type to ZXXX dynamically before the BAPI/API is called.

To ensure this only impacts creation, place the logic within the Structure Mapping specifically for the Sales Order Header of your Create interface. For high-volume O2C flows, I’d also recommend validating these "integration seams" with Tosca API Testing to confirm that the order type conversion doesn't trigger unexpected downstream errors in delivery or billing logic.

For those of us looking toward SAP Sapphire 2026, the "SAP runs SAP" track sounds like a vital resource for understanding how these applications and agents operate in a massive, real-world enterprise environment. Looking forward to seeing these enhancements in action across the May events!

Regarding your question on Revenue Recognition: In the PSA scenario—specifically with Event-Based Revenue Recognition (EBRR)—accruals posted via the Accrual Engine typically do not automatically drive revenue recognition like actual costs do. entry.title: Re: Certification Exam for SAP Certified - SAP Enterprise...
entry.content:
This is a frustrating situation, @mts2605, especially during a high-stakes Enterprise Architect assessment. This "An error occurred" message on the AI-guided roleplay often indicates a session timeout or a conflict with browser-side "integration seams"—specifically with how the AI behavioral feedback module loads its voice and facial expression assets.

Before contacting support, I recommend trying these technical steps:

  • Clear Cache & Cookies: Ensure there are no stale session tokens from the SAP Learning Hub.

  • Check Governance Settings: If you are on a corporate network, ensure that the outgoing API calls for the AI guidance module aren't being blocked by a firewall or governed middleware layer.

  • Use Incognito Mode: This often bypasses extension conflicts that might interfere with the AI roleplay logic.

If the issue persists, I’d suggest documenting the exact timestamp and error log for the SAP Learning support team to ensure your exam attempt is credited back. Good luck getting this resolved!

entry.title: Re: Document Info Records Lab Field
entry.content:
In the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, the "Laboratory" field is no longer a standard header-level field in the Manage Document Info Records app.

To achieve this functionality, the recommended "Clean Core" approach is to use Characteristics and Classes (Classification System). You can define a characteristic for "Laboratory" and assign it to the Class Type 017 (Document Management). This allows your client to maintain and search for documents by lab/design office without requiring the legacy ECC field.

If they specifically need this to sync with the Material Master Lab/Office field, you might consider a BDP (Business Design Profession) logic or a simple custom field extension via the Custom Fields and Logic app to ensure your integration seams remain stable across your PLM processes.

entry.title: Re: Outboound delivery WOCR UNDE
entry.content:
This is a classic EWM determination issue, @basha_shaik7. Even though your screenshot shows that Activity Area R001 and Activity PICK are triggering correctly, the fact that the system falls back to UNDE (Undefined) means it is failing the final validation of your rule GR01.

Since you aren't using packing profiles or filters, the most likely culprit is that the Warehouse Tasks (WTs) being created do not meet the "Min" or "Max" limits defined inside the configuration of Rule GR01. If the WT quantity or weight falls outside the bounds of that specific rule, EWM rejects it and assigns the system default UNDE.

Check these three things:

  • WOCR Limits: Go to Define Warehouse Order Creation Rules and verify the "Min. Items" or "Weight/Volume" limits for GR01. If the current WT is too small, the rule won't apply.

  • Sort Sequence: Ensure that the sort sequence assigned to R001 for the PICK activity is correctly generating the queue.

  • Clean Core Validation: To maintain system stability, I recommend using SAP Cloud ALM to trace these determination failures and validating the "integration seams" with Tosca API Testing to ensure that upstream delivery data isn't missing a parameter required by your WOCR.

entry.title: Re: SAP Concur Integration: Follow SAP Activate Roadmap for Your Cloud Transformation
entry.content:
This is an essential reminder for any organization navigating the move from ECC to S/4HANA, @GabrielaBdL! Relying on SAP Activate and the Fit-to-Standard workshops is the most effective way to ensure that "integration seams" for Concur stay aligned with a Clean Core strategy.

A few key points that make this roadmap approach particularly valuable:

  • Documentation Hierarchy: Referring to SAP Note 3483312 is vital for understanding the nuances when transitioning specifically to the Public Cloud (GROW) model.

  • Quality Gates: Following the structured quality gates in Activate helps prevent the "loss of institutional knowledge" that often occurs during rapid ERP migrations.

  • Automated Validation: For those of us using Tricentis Tosca for continuous testing, these roadmap activities provide the perfect baseline for creating automated test cases that validate the SAP ICS capabilities across every release.

Excellent compilation of resources and SAP Notes!

entry.title: Re: ABAP RAP: Multiple Action Groups in Fiori Elements App
entry.content:
This is a known limitation in the current SADL designtime, @Romanos. The warning SADL_GW_DT_CDS(093) confirms that while the CDS syntax allows multiple #FOR_ACTION_GROUP entries, the metadata engine is only equipped to process one group per collection.

To maintain a "Clean Core" and avoid UI inconsistencies, I recommend consolidating into one primary action group and placing your high-priority actions directly on the toolbar as standalone buttons. If you are scaling this for complex treeviews, you might consider validating the "integration seams" with Tosca API Testing to ensure that your single group remains robust across different service releases.

entry.title: MRP Rescheduling Creates New Purchase Order Instead of Rescheduling Existing PO Line
entry.content:
Hi all,

SAP is not behaving as expected in MRP rescheduling.

Instead of proposing a reschedule of the existing purchase order item 4500012443 line 9 to 02/06/2026, SAP suggests creating a new purchase order for 31/07/2026 and then rescheduling this new order to 02/06/2026.

Is this standard MRP behavior, or is there a configuration issue causing SAP to create a new PO instead of rescheduling the existing one?

Has anyone experienced this behavior in S/4HANA?

Many thanks,

Alice

entry.title: Re: Dunning sample print was sent to customer
entry.content:
This is a stressful situation for any finance team, @FabianSCC. In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, the issue often stems from the Output Parameter Determination (OPD) rules being set to trigger an "Email" channel automatically whenever the "Print" action is initiated, even for sample runs.

To prevent this from happening again, you should adjust your output settings to ensure sample printouts are restricted to a local print spool or a safe preview:

Corrective Steps in Output Parameter Determination (OPD)

  • Check the Dispatch Time: In the OPD transaction, look at the Dispatch Time rule for the Dunning document. If it is set to "1 (Immediately)", the system will trigger the email as soon as the print job is scheduled. Consider changing this to "2 (Scheduled)" for specific test users or environments.

  • Restrict the "Email" Channel: Review your Channel determination rules. You can add a condition that checks for a specific "Output Type" or user role; for sample testing, ensure the channel is forced to "PRINT" or "PREVIEW" only, bypassing the email logic.

  • Use a Dummy Email Address: For the quality/test system, it is a "Clean Core" best practice to use the Email Office configuration to redirect all outgoing emails to a single internal "dummy" address rather than actual customers.

Operational Tip for Clients

In the Schedule Sample Printout pop-up, advise users to click "Display" instead of "Print". "Display" generates a PDF preview without triggering the output management dispatch rules, keeping your customer communication safe.

For long-term governance, I recommend using SAP Cloud ALM to document these output rules and validating the "integration seams" with Tricentis Tosca to ensure that email triggers are disabled during automated testing cycles.

Specifically, we are interested in:

  • Recommended SAP GUI client settings that are known to improve performance or stability in Citrix environments

Finally, could you share, even at a high level, an indicative effort in days for setting up Foundational Data Sync, Create Job from Position (basic setup), more advanced field mapping between SuccessFactors and SmartRecruiters, and ONB integration if applicable? entry.title: Restriction on Batch Determination Based on Manufacturing Date
entry.content:
We are currently operating on SAP S/4HANA 2021, where the business process allows goods issue using movement type 601 through sales orders and outbound deliveries.

However, a system inconsistency has been observed in the batch determination process. Specifically, the system is allowing the creation of sales orders and delivery documents with batch selection even when the batch manufacturing (production) date is later than the transaction date.

For example, a sales transaction is being processed on 01.02.2026, whereas the selected batch has a manufacturing date of 04.02.2026. This indicates that the system is permitting the allocation and picking of stock that is not yet produced, which is logically and operationally incorrect.

This gap can lead to:

  • Incorrect inventory commitments
  • Compliance and audit issues
  • Misalignment between physical stock and system stock
  • Potential financial and legal discrepancies

Business Requirement:

The system must enforce a control to ensure that:

  • Batch determination should not consider or propose any batch where the manufacturing date is greater than the sales order date or delivery creation date.
  • During sales order creation (VA01) and delivery processing (VL01N / batch picking), such future-dated batches must be automatically excluded from selection.
  • The restriction should work consistently across all relevant processes including ATP, batch determination, and goods issue.

Expected Outcome:

  • Only batches with a manufacturing date less than or equal to the transaction date should be eligible for selection.
  • The system should prevent users from manually overriding and selecting future-dated batches.
  • This validation should ensure alignment with actual production availability and maintain audit compliance.

entry.title: Re: SAP B1 Alerts

Hi @mouaz_altabbaa974011, this is a common encoding issue where the mail relay doesn't recognize the UTF-8 Arabic characters and reverts to ??????.

First, check your Job Service Configurator and ensure the SMTP encoding is explicitly set to UTF-8. For a more robust "Clean Core" setup, you might consider routing these alerts through the SAP Alert Notification service on BTP, which provides better native support for multi-language character sets and allows you to validate the payload through Tosca API Testing before it reaches the customer.

entry.title: What is the standard Supplier Confirmation process in SAP IBP (PA – SAPIBP2) ? Any std KF available?
entry.content:
Use Case for Supplier Forecast and Confirmation with SAP IBP (Planning Area – HPA)

Case: The company publishes finished goods forecasts to suppliers.  Suppliers are modeled as locations in IBP to enable capacity planning at supplier product level.

An unconstrained Order-Based Planning (OBP) run is executed to generate total demand-driven capacity requirements at the supplier location, providing visibility of required vs available capacity. Based on this, the supplier reviews the load and updates feasible supply commitments at supplier product level.

Subsequently, a constrained OBP run is executed incorporating supplier capacity limits and commitments, resulting in a realistic, capacity-feasible supply plan aligned with supplier capabilities.

This process enables improved collaboration, capacity visibility, and achievable supply commitments between OEM and suppliers.

Challenge 1:  

  • There is no standard key figure (Marked Red box) available in SAP IBP to represent the supplier-facing forecast that can be shared with suppliers in alignment with best practices.
  • Similarly, there is no standard key figure available to capture supplier confirmations/commitments as an input for the constrained planning run in line with best practices.

Existing  Process Flow :

 

 

 

 

 

Challenge 2

  • As shown in the screenshot, capacity consumption from planned and production orders is available only at the resource level, where multiple products share the same resource. However, in the current HPA Order-Based Planning setup, it is not possible to constrain the production plan specifically at the resource, location, and product combination level.

entry.title: Re: SmartRecruiters / SuccessFactors – Integration scopin...
entry.content:
Hi Barbara, these are excellent scoping questions. To address your core technical points:

  • Independence of Integrations: Yes, you can absolutely deploy Foundational Data Sync (FDS) independently of the Create Job from Position integration. If your client prefers creating requisitions directly in SmartRecruiters, FDS ensures that the picklists and organizational values (Company, Department, Location) remain synchronized with SuccessFactors as the system of record.

  • Prefilling Fields: When creating jobs directly in SmartRecruiters without Position Management, fields are typically populated by selecting from the synced reference values. To "automatically" prefill without a position link, you would usually rely on SmartRecruiters Job Templates or custom mapping logic in your middleware (like SAP BTP or Boomi) to default values based on the recruiter's business unit or the chosen Job Profile.

  • Workstream Separation: You are correct—Employee Central (Core HR) and Onboarding (ONB) are distinct workstreams with different API endpoints, permission sets, and data objects (Job Information vs. Onboarding Candidate Info).

  • Indicative Effort (Rough Ranges):

    • Foundational Data Sync: 3–5 days (basic setup and picklist alignment).

    • Create Job from Position: 5–10 days (requires Position Management alignment).

    • Advanced Field Mapping: 5–15 days (depends heavily on the number of custom fields and transformation logic).

    • Onboarding Integration: 10–20 days (complex due to data privacy, document triggers, and cross-system validation).

For long-term governance, I recommend using SAP Cloud ALM to track these separate workstreams and validating the "integration seams" via Tricentis Tosca to ensure that SuccessFactors releases don't disrupt your custom field mappings.

entry.title: SAP standard function modules which are in not released status
entry.content:
Hello,

Following our recent upgrade from ECC to S/4HANA, we’ve identified instances where our custom code calls SAP function module VKK_FICA_DI that are not released for customer use. We would appreciate SAP’s guidance on suitable, released alternatives that we can adopt in our custom developments.

Could you please advise on:

  • Recommended released APIs (e.g., BAPIs, fubctions, released classes/methods, or other whitelisted interfaces) that can replace the unreleased function modules
  • Any standard tools or documentation to help map unreleased function modules to released equivalents

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: SAP standard function modules which are in not releas...
entry.content:
Hello,

thank you for your request regarding non-released function modules.

For your use case, I recommend using the BAPI_CTRACCONTRACTACCOUNT_CH1 instead of the non-released function module VKK_FICA_DI. General information about non-released function modules can be found in SAP Note 109533.

To identify related released BAPIs, you have two options:

  • Use the where-used list in transaction SE37 by entering the function module to be replaced (e.g., VKK_FICA_DI) and search for "BAPI" in the results.
  • Use transaction SE37 with F4-help to search for released BAPI function modules by name pattern (e.g., BAPI_CTRAC* in contract accounting).

Alternatively, you can contact your implementation partner for further guidance on released objects available for customer use.

Best regards,
Ronny
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: MRP Rescheduling Creates New Purchase Order Instead o...

Hi @alice_henrique70a956, this often happens if the requirement date is outside your Rescheduling Horizon. MRP sees a shortage on 02/06, but because the PO is on 01/07, it may be outside the 'window' where the system is allowed to pull receipts forward.

Check your horizon settings in SSCUI 102144 (for Public Cloud) or OMDT (for Private). Also, to maintain a Clean Core, I'd recommend validating this logic using Tricentis Tosca to ensure that your lot-sizing and horizons remain consistent after S/4HANA upgrades.

entry.title: Re: S_ALR_87013336 dumps with SQL parser error after upgr...
entry.content:
Hi @Balamut, this SQL parser error often occurs because the generated ABAP code from your 1809 system is no longer compatible with the unified table structures in S/4HANA 2025.

To fix this, go to transaction GR55, enter your report group, and select Generate from the menu. This will refresh the dynamic SQL to match the current database schema. I also recommend checking SAP Note 2999120, which specifically addresses SQL parser issues in Report Writer after major upgrades.

Moving forward, it’s a "Clean Core" best practice to use SAP Cloud ALM to track these report regenerations as part of your upgrade cutover plan and to validate them using Tricentis Tosca to ensure they stay functional through future feature pack updates.

entry.content:
Hi @Balamut, your suspicion is spot on. In S/4HANA 2025, FAGL_LINE_ITEM_BROWSER leverages ALV IDA, which processes data on the database layer and bypasses the hooks used by CL_SALV_BS_RUNTIME_INFO.

To stay aligned with a Clean Core strategy, I recommend moving away from the SUBMIT approach. Instead, consume the underlying CDS Views directly in your ABAP code or use the Fiori-native APIs provided for G/L line items.

If you must use the report logic, check if there is an available BAPI or a Public Class that provides the data. You can also use SAP Cloud ALM to document this technical debt and validate the new data retrieval logic with Tricentis Tosca to ensure it remains stable across future S/4HANA releases.

entry.title: LIST_TO_ASCI no longer works with ABAPLIST in EHP7
entry.content:
Hi everyone.
I have the following function in a program that worked fine in R3 and ECC6, but now that I'm trying it in EHP7, it gives me an error.
 
  DATA  BEGIN OF v_asci OCCURS 0.
          INCLUDE STRUCTURE abaplist.
  DATA  END OF v_asci.
  
  DATA: mem_tab TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF abaplist.
 
[...]
  
    CALL FUNCTION 'LIST_FROM_MEMORY'
      TABLES
        listobject = mem_tab
      EXCEPTIONS
        not_found = 1
        others = 2.
    IF not mem_tab[] is initial.
      REFRESH v_asci.
      CALL FUNCTION 'LIST_TO_ASCI'
*        EXPORTING
*          CONVERT_SYMB_AND_ICON_2_TEXT = abap_true
        TABLES
          listasci   = v_asci
          listobject = mem_tab
        EXCEPTIONS
          empty_list = 1
          list_index_invalid = 2
          others = 3.
  
[...]
  
 
The error it gives me is that "listasci" must be a pure text table.
I saw that compared to the previous release, there's an extra parameter: CONVERT_SYMB_AND_ICON_2_TEXT. I tried setting it in abap_true, but it doesn't change anything; it gives me the same error.
 
Do I have to create a text-only table?
I tried that too, but then I can't get an abaplist table like before.
 
What can I do?
I'd like to change as little code as possible.

entry.title: Re: Updating Stock Type / System Status in Material Seria...
entry.content:
Hi @edoymaner, you've hit a standard system constraint. Fields like Stock Type and System Status are strictly transaction-driven to maintain a Clean Core.

You cannot PATCH them directly because they must stay in sync with the physical inventory. Instead of the Serial Number API, use the API_MATERIAL_DOCUMENT to post a transfer or movement. When you include the serial number in the material document payload, SAP will update those fields for you automatically. For long-term governance, I'd recommend tracking these status transitions in SAP Cloud ALM and validating the flow with Tosca API Testing to ensure no "orphan" serial numbers are created during high-volume movements.

Hi @JoseGuill, I completely understand your frustration—this is a high-stakes "Clean Core" issue where the platform's internal mapping appears to be broken in your Production tenant.

The double space in the error usually means the variable for the Type Name is initial (null) at runtime. This often happens if the AIF Metadata or the eDocument Proxy wasn't fully activated during the production cutover.

Have you verified if there is a difference in the Feature Toggle status between the two systems? Sometimes a specific KSeF "Early Adopter" toggle is active in Test but was missed in Production. I’d also recommend using SAP Cloud ALM to document this discrepancy and escalate it as a "System Inconsistency" rather than a configuration error to get it routed to the development team faster.

entry.title: Re: Is this a sign SAP is losing the AI race?
entry.content:
This is a powerful analysis, @joao_sousa2. You've identified the core challenge: the difference between technical governance and market protectionism.

If Joule is to succeed, it must become an "Expert Guide" that adds value general LLMs can't touch, such as native understanding of complex SAP financial and logistics structures. Mandating Joule via A2A protocols might solve the stability issue, but it risks alienating developers who are already seeing massive productivity gains from "open" AI tools like Claude.

Moving forward, the most successful implementations will likely be those that use SAP Cloud ALM to govern these new "integration seams" while pushing SAP to make Joule more capable through deep-tier skills and better support for local/hybrid AI models.

entry.title: Re: Building Custom Joule Capabilities: Role-Based Scenario Access in Joule
entry.content:
This is a masterclass in implementing "Clean Core" security principles for AI extensions, @david_bizer!

Using the visibility_condition field to gate scenarios based on IAS identity tokens is far superior to trying to handle permissions within the function logic or via the LLM prompt. It prevents "intent leaking" where a user might discover the existence of a restricted scenario simply by asking the right questions.

A few key takeaways for those implementing this:

  • Implicit AND: Remember that adding multiple objects to the visibility_condition array creates an implicit AND condition—excellent for cross-validating group membership with specific Work Zone navigation targets.

  • Attribute Versatility: Beyond just groups, the support for user_uuid and email is a lifesaver for designated testing phases or managing high-privilege capability owners.

  • Session Latency: As noted, since these attributes are baked into the JWT at login, users might need to re-authenticate after an IAS group change to see the new scenarios in Joule.

This declarative approach keeps the "integration seams" between Joule and IAS robust and manageable. Looking forward to the next part on Document Grounding!

entry.title: Re: Billing Split issue solution order management
entry.content:
This is a common challenge with the #4GT scope item, @alexbeer! The split occurs because the "integration seams" between the Service BDR and the Sales Delivery don't share identical header fields like Customer Reference or Assignment by default.

To resolve this without breaking your Clean Core, you should indeed use the Cloud BAdI SD_BIL_DATA_TRANSFER. This allows you to programmatically align the header fields (like VBRK-BSTNK) so the system perceives them as a single logical transaction.

For long-term governance, I recommend using SAP Cloud ALM to document this custom logic and validating it with Tricentis Tosca to ensure that future S/4HANA upgrades don't revert your header alignment and re-introduce the splits.

entry.title: Re: Esri ArcGIS license requirement for SAP Spatial asset...
entry.content:
Hi @shirsendu_roy2, you are on the right track. An Esri ArcGIS license is not mandatory for SAP Spatial Asset Management.

If your requirements are met by OpenStreetMap and the Spatial Data Explorer, you can run a "GIS-agnostic" architecture where SAP HANA acts as the primary spatial database. You only need ArcGIS integration (and services like Sync4GEF) if your business requires specialized GIS functionalities like advanced topological editing or network trace analysis that OpenStreetMap cannot provide.

To maintain a Clean Core, I recommend sticking with your current OSM approach and using SAP Cloud ALM to govern the spatial data structures in HANA. You can also validate your spatial work orders in SSAM using Tricentis Tosca to ensure the map coordinates remain accurate across mobile updates.

You must update the password in two places: first in the SAP Cloud Connector (under Resources) and then within the SAC Connection settings. entry.title: Re: Custom Business Configurator . Entries shows as Draft...
entry.content:
Hi @Aravindkurup, this usually happens when the Draft Administrative Data is not being cleared after the "Activate" action in your RAP business object.

Even though the data hits your Z-table, the UI keeps the draft state if the Total ETag or the Prepare action hasn't successfully signaled the end of the draft lifecycle. Check your Behavior Definition (BDEF) to ensure the draft determine action Prepare is correctly implemented and that your draft table is properly linked.

To maintain a Clean Core, I'd recommend using SAP Cloud ALM to track these configuration lifecycle issues and validating the UI state transitions with Tricentis Tosca to ensure the "Draft" indicator clears correctly across different environment updates.

To help you narrow this down, does the "Draft" indicator disappear if you manually refresh the browser, or does it stay there permanently?

Using a decentralized ledger like Hyperledger to ground this data before it hits the Maintenance (PM) or Transportation Management (TM) modules is a robust way to maintain a Clean Core while enabling true predictive failure analysis.

I’m currently exploring how this convergence reduces 'blind spots' in logistics execution. I’d be interested to hear your thoughts on how SAP BTP can further orchestrate these high-frequency data streams without compromising system stability.

entry.title: Re: PO Archive
entry.content:
Hi @youssef_hassnien, based on your screenshots, you are at the final hurdle! TheDelete step will only show files if the Write job finished successfully without errors.

A common 'blind spot' in MM_EKKO archiving is the residence time setting in transaction OLME—if the documents haven't reached this age, the Preprocessing job will ignore them.

To maintain a Clean Core, I recommend using SAP Cloud ALM to monitor your archiving health and ensuring that your 'integration seams' with external storage are validated. You can also use Tricentis Tosca to automate the verification that archived POs are still accessible via the Archive Information System (SARI) after the deletion is complete.

entry.title: Re: Restriction on Batch Determination Based on Manufactu...
entry.content:
Hi @yasovandanroop_talluri, this is a common gap because standard Batch Determination often prioritizes the SLED over the Manufacturing Date (HSDAT).

To fix this without custom code, you should update your Selection Criteria in the Batch Search Strategy (Transaction VCH1 for SD or MBC1 for MM). By using the standard characteristic LOBM_HSDAT and setting a selection rule where the value must be less than or equal to the current date, the system will automatically filter out those "future" batches.

For long-term audit compliance and to maintain a Clean Core, I recommend documenting this logic in SAP Cloud ALM and using Tricentis Tosca to automate a 'negative test'—confirming that a future-dated batch cannot be picked even if manually entered. This ensures your 'integration seams' between Production and SD remain logically sound.

One follow-up question: are these future-dated batches being created manually, or are they coming from Advanced Planning (e.g., PP/DS) where the production date is estimated?

entry.title: Beyond the Basics: Top 10 Best Practices for Advanced Carbon Accounting in 2026
entry.content:
Top 10 Best Practices for Carbon & Environmental Accounting

  1. Transition from "Estimated" to "Actual" Data: Move away from spend-based averages. Use primary activity data (e.g., actual material weights and energy consumption) directly from your ERP to ensure precision.

  2. Adopt the "Green Ledger" Mindset: Treat carbon like cash. Integrate CO2e into your financial chart of accounts to ensure double-entry rigor and auditability.

  3. Ensure 95% Scope 3 Coverage: In line with 2026 GHG Protocol updates, ensure you aren't just reporting the "easy" categories. Identify and fill data gaps in Category 1 (Purchased Goods) and Category 11 (Use of Sold Products).

  4. Avoid the "Carbon Tunnel Vision": Implement Multi-Impact assessments. Monitor water scarcity, land use, and acidification alongside CO2 to prevent "burden shifting" during product optimization.

  5. Automate ERP Integration: Eliminate manual spreadsheets. Use SAP Sustainability Footprint Management to automatically link emission factors to your material masters and production orders.

  6. Direct Supplier Data Exchange: Stop guessing your Scope 3. Use the SAP Sustainability Data Exchange to receive actual Product Carbon Footprint (PCF) data from suppliers via standardized protocols.

  7. Granular Scope 2 Reporting: For electricity, move toward matching consumption with renewable energy certificates on an hourly and regional basis to reflect true grid impact.

  8. AI-Powered Factor Mapping: Leverage AI to map thousands of ERP material lines to scientific emission databases (like ecoinvent), reducing manual effort and human error.

  9. Predictive Footprinting: Don’t just report the past. Use "What-if" simulations to see how changing a raw material or a logistics route affects the final product footprint before the decision is made.

  10. Establish a Digital Audit Trail: Ensure every gram of CO2 can be traced back to its source. A "Single Source of Truth" in the SAP Sustainability Control Tower is essential for CSRD compliance.

#SAP #Sustainability #CarbonAccounting #ESG #GreenLedger #DigitalTransformation #LearningJourney

entry.title: Requirement for Complete Delivery per Sales Order for a particular customer
entry.content:
Hello All,

I have a requirement to ensure that deliveries to the customer are completed in a single shipment.

For example, if a sales order contains two line items with quantities of 5 and 6, the customer does not want partial deliveries. Instead, a single delivery should be created only when both line items (5 and 6 quantities) can be fulfilled together.

I am aware of the “Complete Delivery” indicator in the customer master, and I have already enabled it. However, this setting appears to control completion at the line item level only. Currently, the system still allows the creation of separate deliveries for individual line items, even though it prevents reducing the requested quantity within a line item.

Could you please advise how we can enforce complete delivery at the entire sales order level, so that delivery creation is only possible when all line items are ready?

Thanks in advance for your guidance.

Best regards,
Aravind

entry.title: From Concept to Practice: Inside My SAP Business Data Cloud Session for Data Teams (BDC2853)
entry.content:
Hi everyone—Kara here 👋

SAPPHIRE is just a few days away, which means a lot of great conversations about data, AI, and how the two actually come together in the real world (not just on slides).

A bit about me: I spend my time at SAP working on all things data and AI - specifically how to turn all the data customers already have into something that actually drives decisions, powers AI, and (ideally) makes life a little easier. A big part of that lately has been helping teams think through what a solid data foundation really looks like when AI is no longer “experimental” – it’s expected.

I am excited to share a bit about my session: SAP Business Data Cloud: Deep dive on business capabilities | BDC2853

If you’ve heard about SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC) but are still wondering “okay…but what does it *actually* do for me?”—this session is for you.

We’ll walk through:

  • What’s inside BDC (without the buzzword overload)
  • How the different pieces come together across data, analytics, and AI
  • Who it’s really for – because “everyone” is not a useful answer
  • How key capabilities work in action through short demos

My goal is simple: you should walk away knowing how to connect what BDC offers to your day-to-day work and not just understanding the architecture diagram. Also, good news: you don’t have to be in Orlando to join – this session is available virtually too.

If you will be in Orlando and are already thinking about your data + AI strategy, here are a few other sessions worth checking out: 

      • The data foundation for agentic AI: Implementing a business data fabric | BDC2058V
      • The semantic universe: Dole’s data fabric strategy from farm to fork | BDC2742
      • How Baker Hughes uses SAP BDC and SAP Databricks for AI-enabled operations | PAR1039

Your turn 👇

Before SAPPHIRE kicks off, I’m curious: What’s the hardest part of getting your data ready for AI right now? Is it:

  1. Data quality and governance?
  2. Integration across systems?
  3. Rebuilding business context after replication?
  4. Or something else entirely?

Drop it in the comments—I’d love to hear what’s top of mind (and what’s getting in the way).

Looking forward to seeing you—whether in Orlando or online!

entry.title: Re: Is this a sign SAP is losing the AI race?
entry.content:
@JRAS thanks! It’s actually ironic that with all the push for clean core, the moment external API calls start getting traction beyond the BTP space SAP seem to say “clean core … but not like this”. 

Many companies still use ABAP development because it’s honestly cheaper, but now general use agentic programming tools could bridge that gap and actually make customers go cleaner core as non ABAP costs are even lower. 

Can you use those tools and deploy them to BTP? Sure, but then you go back to proprietary stack with limited knowledge in the market and there goes the cost reduction.

entry.title: Agentic AI and the Future of Finance
entry.content:
SAP's finance applications have transformed from traditional ERP systems into intelligent, cloud-native platforms that deliver real-time insights and strategic value to modern enterprises.  The evolution reflects a fundamental transition from transactional back-office processing to strategic business enablement, driven by CFOs' demand for real-time visibility, predictive analytics, and AI driven automation.

Finance departments have benefited from AI and Automation for years.  The earliest example is rules-based systems supported by analytics.  These systems were used for credit scoring, accounting reconciliation and fraud detection.  They basically relied on “if-then” logic to enforce static parameters.

The introduction of statistical modeling and machine learning provided more intelligence by incorporating forecasting and other predictive analytic algorithms to determine the probability or propensity of an outcome.  This helped tremendously to identify what new types of behaviors could indicate fraud.

Natural language processing is the foundation for generative AI.  By connecting to Large Language Models (LLMs), this capability enables you to have a conversation with your data.  This helps to describe what is possibly making new activity fraudulent or identify root causes of anomalies in your financial analysis. Based on the outcomes, semi-autonomous agents can execute the appropriate actions. Based on the results, semi-autonomous agents can execute the appropriate actions. 

Evolution of AI and Automation

This brings us to the holy grail – human managed agent-driven autonomous enterprise.

Future Vision of AI and Automation

 All the previous stages do not disappear. The evolve or continue but will eventually be managed by the agents.   This encourages you to get started now to get the incremental benefits of the individual stages.Sample Benefits

 As you can see in the samples, financial benefit has increased over time.  One of the biggest factors impacting the value realized from AI is the use case.  It is great to have experiments in labs to familiarize your teams with the technology, but the real impact comes from applying Agentic AI to core enterprise processes.

Example of Inter-departmental Processes

 

We have all heard that data is the new oil.  Just like your car needs the right viscosity. AI needs the right data. Connecting your systems requires more than just a wire.  Whether it is a human, an automated process, or an agent, there must be clean, real-time data. The APIs must be understandable and support consistent business semantics from all perspectives. 

If you don’t know where you are going any road will take you there. Financial Planning and Analysis (FP&A) provides the baseline of where your company is now and the guidance to align teams for success.

Analysis of current processes will determine if they are aligned to the objectives identified in the financial plan. Automation of the optimized processes will enhance efficiencies.  Embedded AI and custom agents will take automated processes to the next level.

One of the key components of effective AI is business context.  Having the proper semantics for agents and skills to communicate effectively is paramount to success.

And last but certainly not least is AI Governance. Robust AI Governance provides the visibility and control of process execution. 

Platform Capabilities for your Autonomous Journey

 

Integration SuiteFinancial Planning & AnalysisProcess MiningJoule StudioCustom AIBusiness Data CloudAI Governance

To learn more please visit the links or come hear Accenture speak at Sapphire about their journey to the intelligent enterprise.

Sapphire Orlando 2026
